english: Excuse me, can we have the bill?
choicesEnglish: Excuse me, can we have the bill?
Excuse me, can we lose the way?
Excuse me, can we laugh at the news?
Excuse me, can we run the tour?
correctAnswerEnglish: Excuse me, could we have the check?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Il modo più comune e cortese per chiedere quanto pagare.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai chiedendo il documento per saldare il pasto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! The most common and polite way to ask how much to pay.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're asking for the document to settle the meal.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! La risposta rassicurante del cameriere.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica un'azione immediata di servizio.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! The waiter's reassuring response.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates an immediate service action.

choicesEnglish: Can we lose with the bag?
Can we laugh with the news?
Can we run with the trip?
correctAnswerEnglish: Can we pay with a card?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Verificare la disponibilità del pagamento elettronico.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi sapere se accettano pagamenti non in contanti.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Verifying the availability of electronic payment.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want to know if they accept non-cash payments.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Confermare che ogni circuito di pagamento è valido.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indichi una flessibilità totale sui metodi di pagamento.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Confirming that every payment circuit is valid.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you indicate total flexibility on payment methods.

english: Do you bring the card reader to the table?
choicesEnglish: Do you bring the terminal to the table?
Do you lose the bag at the port?
Do you laugh at the news to the king?
Do you run the trip to the tower?
correctAnswerEnglish: Do you bring the card machine to the table?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Chiedere se è possibile pagare senza alzarsi.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, cerchi lo strumento per la transazione elettronica al tuo posto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Asking if it's possible to pay without getting up.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're looking for the electronic transaction tool at your seat.

english: You (plural) must go to the cash desk.
choicesEnglish: You have to go to the counter.
You have to lose the way.
You have to laugh at the note.
You have to run the port.
correctAnswerEnglish: You need to pay at the register.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Indicare il luogo obbligatorio per il pagamento.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, il personale ti invita a recarti dove c'è il registratore.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Indicating the mandatory place for payment.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the staff invites you to go where the register is.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Chiedere il debito totale tra amici o al cameriere.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi sapere la cifra complessiva da versare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Asking for the total debt among friends or to the waiter.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want to know the total amount to be paid.

italian: Il totale è di ottanta euro.
choicesItalian: Il totale è di ottanta euro.
Lo sporco è di ottanta metri.
Il finto è di ottanta baci.
Il saggio è di ottanta tour.
correctAnswerItalian: Il totale è di ottanta euro.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Comunicare la cifra finale del conto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indichi la somma monetaria complessiva.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Communicating the final bill figure.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you indicate the overall monetary sum.

choicesEnglish: Shall we lose on the street?
Shall we laugh at the news?
Shall we run on the trip?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Proporre la divisione equa del conto tra i presenti.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è un'espressione gergale per la divisione in parti uguali.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Proposing an equal split of the bill among those present.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's a slang expression for splitting into equal parts.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Chiarire il metodo di spartizione del pagamento.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indichi che ognuno paga la stessa quota.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Clarifying the payment splitting method.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you indicate that everyone pays the same share.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Definire la quota individuale dopo il calcolo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, 'a testa' significa per ciascuna persona.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Defining the individual share after the calculation.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, 'a testa' means per each person.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Dichiarare la propria disponibilità di moneta fisica.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indichi che non possiedi o non vuoi usare carte.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Declaring your availability of physical money.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you indicate that you don't have or don't want to use cards.

english: I'll pay with the debit card at the till.
choicesEnglish: I pay with the card at the counter.
I lose with the mud on the street.
I laugh with the kiss at the news.
I run with the tour on the trip.
correctAnswerEnglish: I'm paying with my card at the desk.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Specificare metodo e luogo del proprio pagamento.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai andando a saldare elettronicamente.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Specifying method and place of your payment.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're going to settle electronically.

italian: Credo ci sia un errore nel conto.
english: I think there's a mistake on the bill.
choicesItalian: Credo ci sia un errore nel conto.
Credo ci sia un fango nella borsa.
Credo ci sia un bacio nella news.
Credo ci sia un tour nella gita.
choicesEnglish: I think there is an error in the bill.
I think there is mud in the bag.
I think there is a kiss in the news.
I think there is a tour in the trip.
correctAnswerItalian: Credo ci sia un errore nel conto.
correctAnswerEnglish: I believe there's a mistake on the check.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Sollevare un dubbio sulla correttezza degli addebiti.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, sospetti che i calcoli o le voci siano sbagliati.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Raising a doubt about the correctness of the charges.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you suspect the calculations or items are wrong.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! La domanda del cameriere per capire il problema.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, chiede spiegazioni sul reclamo appena fatto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! The waiter's question to understand the problem.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, he asks for explanations on the complaint just made.

italian: C'è segnata una bottiglia di vino.
english: A bottle of wine is marked (on the bill).
choicesItalian: C'è segnata una bottiglia di vino.
C'è persa una borsa di fango.
C'è risa una news di re.
C'è corsa una gita di porto.
choicesEnglish: A bottle of wine is marked.
correctAnswerItalian: C'è segnata una bottiglia di vino.
correctAnswerEnglish: There's a bottle of wine listed.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Indicare la voce specifica del conto contestata.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, mostri l'articolo che non dovrebbe esserci.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Indicating the specific contested bill item.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're showing the item that shouldn't be there.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Negare il consumo di un prodotto segnato per errore.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, confermi che quell'articolo non è mai arrivato al tavolo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Denying the consumption of a product marked by mistake.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you confirm that that item never arrived at the table.

choicesEnglish: You are right, excuse me so much.
You have a bag, lose me so much.
You have news, laugh at me so much.
You have a trip, silence me so much.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! L'ammissione dello sbaglio da parte del personale.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è una formula di scuse formali.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! The staff's admission of the mistake.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's a formal apology formula.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Attribuire lo sbaglio a un problema tecnico.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indichi che la colpa è della macchina o del software.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Attributing the mistake to a technical problem.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you indicate the machine or software is at fault.

english: I'll redo the receipt for you (singular polite).
choicesEnglish: I redo the receipt for you.
I lose the bag for you.
I laugh at the news for us.
I run the trip for you.
correctAnswerEnglish: I'll issue a new receipt for you.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Proporre la correzione immediata del documento fiscale.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, il cameriere sta andando a stampare la versione corretta.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Proposing the immediate correction of the tax document.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the waiter is going to print the corrected version.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Chiedere se la prestazione del personale è già nel totale.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi sapere se devi aggiungere soldi per il servizio.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Asking if the staff's performance is already in the total.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want to know if you should add money for service.

english: Cover and service in the total.
choicesEnglish: Cover and service in the total.
Bag and mud in the street.
News and kiss in the king.
Trip and tour in the port.
correctAnswerEnglish: Cover and service charge are in the total.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Confermare che i costi accessori sono già conteggiati.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indichi che non ci sono aggiunte da fare per quegli articoli.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Confirming that the accessory costs are already counted.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you indicate there are no additions to be made for those items.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Lasciare un extra volontario per il personale.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai regalando una somma per il buon lavoro svolto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Leaving a voluntary extra for the staff.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are giving a sum for the good work done.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Motivare la mancia con un complimento.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, lodi la qualità dell'assistenza ricevuta.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Motivating the tip with a compliment.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you praise the quality of the assistance received.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Ricambiare un augurio (es. buona serata).
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è una risposta standard per augurare la stessa cosa all'altro.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Reciprocating a wish (e.g., good evening).
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's a standard response to wish the same thing to the other person.

english: How much do I owe you (singular polite)?
choicesEnglish: How much do I owe you?
correctAnswerEnglish: How much do I owe you?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Chiedere il conto in modo diretto ma educato.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi sapere la cifra da pagare al barista o cameriere.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Asking for the bill in a direct but polite way.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want to know the figure to pay the barista or waiter.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Elencare i prodotti consumati al bar.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, specifichi gli articoli per il calcolo del totale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Listing the products consumed at the bar.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you specify the items for the total calculation.

italian: Tre euro e cinquanta in tutto.
english: Three euros and fifty in all.
choicesItalian: Tre euro e cinquanta in tutto.
Tre metri e fango in via.
Tre baci e news in re.
Tre tour e gita in porto.
choicesEnglish: Three meters and mud on the street.
Three kisses and news for the king.
Three tours and trip at the port.
correctAnswerItalian: Tre euro e cinquanta in tutto.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Comunicare il prezzo complessivo della colazione.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è la risposta del barista sul totale da pagare.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Communicating the total breakfast price.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the barista's response on the total to pay.

english: Here are five euros for you.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Porre la banconota per il pagamento.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, stai dando i soldi fisicamente all'addetto.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Handing over the banknote for payment.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you are physically giving the money to the attendant.

italian: Ecco il resto e lo scontrino.
english: Here is the change and the receipt.
choicesItalian: Ecco il resto e lo scontrino.
Prego il fango e la borsa.
Salve il bacio e la news.
Ciao il porto e il tour.
choicesEnglish: You're welcome the mud and the bag.
Hello the kiss and the news.
Bye the port and the tour.
correctAnswerItalian: Ecco il resto e lo scontrino.
correctAnswerEnglish: Here's your change and the receipt.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Completare l'operazione di pagamento al bancone.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, ricevi indietro i soldi in eccesso e la prova fiscale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Completing the payment operation at the counter.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you receive back the excess money and the tax proof.

english: We would like to have separate bills.
choicesEnglish: We'd like to lose dirty bags.
We'd like to laugh at fake news.
We'd like to run wise trips.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Chiedere che ognuno paghi solo il proprio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, rifiuti il conto unico per l'intero gruppo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Asking that everyone pays only their own.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you refuse the single bill for the entire group.

choicesEnglish: We laugh at a fake news.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! La politica del ristorante durante gli orari affollati.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indichi che non è possibile dividere i conti singolarmente.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! The restaurant's policy during busy times.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates that it is not possible to split the bills individually.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Scegliere di saldare il totale in un'unica soluzione.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indichi che una sola persona versa il totale o che vi dividete dopo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Choosing to settle the total in a single solution.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you indicate that one person pays the total or that you'll split later.

correctAnswerEnglish: We'll sort it out among ourselves later.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Gestire la spartizione dei soldi internamente al gruppo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, rassicuri il cameriere che vi dividerete il debito tra voi.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Managing the money split internally within the group.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you reassure the waiter that you'll divide the debt among yourselves.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Formula di ringraziamento professionale per la pazienza del cliente.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, il cameriere ti ringrazia per aver accettato la sua richiesta.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Professional thank you formula for the customer's patience.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the waiter thanks you for accepting his request.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Segnalare un problema col pagamento elettronico.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indichi che il sistema non ha autorizzato il prelievo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Signaling a problem with the electronic payment.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you indicate that the system did not authorize the withdrawal.

choicesEnglish: Do you laugh at another news?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Chiedere una modalità alternativa di pagamento dopo un fallimento.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, cerchi un secondo bancomat o carta di credito.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Asking for an alternative payment method after a failure.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're looking for a second debit or credit card.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Usare la tecnologia a sfioramento invece del chip.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, tenti un metodo di lettura della carta diverso.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Using touch technology instead of the chip.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're trying a different card reading method.

correctAnswerEnglish: It went through now. Thank you.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Confermare il successo del pagamento.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indichi che la transazione è stata finalmente accettata.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Confirming the payment success.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you indicate that the transaction was finally accepted.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Esclamazione di sollievo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, esprimi contentezza per lo scampato pericolo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Exclamation of relief.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you express happiness for the narrow escape.

choicesEnglish: A kiss laughs at the news.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Gesto di ospitalità tipico a fine pasto in Italia.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, il ristorante ti regala il liquore digestivo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Typical hospitality gesture at the end of a meal in Italy.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, the restaurant gives you the digestive liqueur for free.

correctAnswerEnglish: We are ready to settle up.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Segnalare la volontà di chiudere il conto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indichi che avete finito e volete andare via.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Signaling the will to close the bill.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you indicate that you've finished and want to leave.

choicesEnglish: Do you lose a dirty bag?
Do you laugh at fake news?
Do you run a long trip?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Chiedere se un circuito specifico è accettato.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, verifichi la compatibilità di una carta specifica.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Asking if a specific circuit is accepted.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you verify the compatibility of a specific card.

italian: Mi fa lo scontrino, per favore?
english: Can you give me the receipt, please?
choicesItalian: Mi fa lo scontrino, per favore?
choicesEnglish: Can I have the receipt, please?
Can you lose the way for me, thanks?
Can you give back the news, please?
Can you silence the port, hello?
correctAnswerItalian: Mi fa lo scontrino, per favore?
correctAnswerEnglish: Could you give me the receipt, please?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Richiedere il documento fiscale obbligatorio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, vuoi la prova cartacea del pagamento.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Requesting the mandatory tax document.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you want the paper proof of payment.

english: Can I have the tax receipt?
choicesEnglish: Can I have the tax receipt?
Can I lose the dirty bag?
Can I laugh at the fake news?
Can I run the long trip?
correctAnswerEnglish: May I have a formal receipt?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Richiedere un documento più dettagliato dello scontrino.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è una richiesta formale per scopi amministrativi.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Requesting a more detailed document than the receipt.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's a formal request for administrative purposes.

choicesEnglish: Go ahead and lose on the street.
Go ahead and laugh at the news.
Go ahead and run on the trip.
correctAnswerEnglish: Please come to the front desk.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Invito del personale a recarsi al punto di pagamento.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, indica che il pagamento non si fa al tavolo.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! The staff's invitation to go to the payment point.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it indicates payment is not done at the table.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Confermare che non ci sono errori nel calcolo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, approvi la cifra presentata dal cameriere.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Confirming that there are no calculation errors.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you approve the figure presented by the waiter.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Dichiarare di voler pagare per tutti.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è un gesto generoso verso gli amici.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Declaring the intention to pay for everyone.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's a generous gesture toward friends.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Specificare il turno di pagamento in un gruppo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, ti prendi la responsabilità del conto per questa occasione.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Specifying the payment turn in a group.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, you're taking responsibility for the bill for this occasion.

english: Goodbye and have a good day.
choicesEnglish: See you soon and good night.
Bye and have a good workday.
correctAnswerEnglish: Goodbye and have a nice day.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Il saluto finale dopo aver completato tutto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, è il congedo standard dal locale.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! The final greeting after completing everything.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it's the standard farewell from the place.
